articles.title: BBC Crime Drama 'From Darkness' Filmed in Rochdale Now Available to Stream
articles.excerpt: The BBC crime drama 'From Darkness', filmed in Rochdale, is now available to stream for free, featuring a gripping storyline and a talented cast.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 20 July 2026 at 07:01. 4 source updates have been combined into this article.

The BBC crime drama 'From Darkness', filmed in Rochdale, is now available to stream for free, featuring a gripping storyline and a talented cast.

Latest update

The BBC crime drama 'From Darkness', filmed in Rochdale, is now available to stream for free, featuring a gripping storyline and a talented cast.

A BBC crime drama called 'From Darkness', filmed in Rochdale, is currently available to stream for free on the platform U. The series, which first aired in 2015, follows former police officer Claire Church, portrayed by Anne-Marie Duff, as she grapples with an unsolved serial killer case and her own troubled past.

The show features various locations, including Lower Falinge in Rochdale and Manchester. The narrative centres around Claire, who, after leaving Manchester due to the unsettling nature of a case she could not solve, is drawn back to the city and her former partner, DCI John Hind, played by Johnny Harris. The cast also includes notable actors such as Caroline Lee-Johnson, Luke Newberry, and Richard Rankin.

The plot thickens as two victims are discovered at a Manchester building site, linked to the serial killer Claire had previously investigated. The series explores her efforts to piece together clues from both the past and present to confront the killer and her own demons.

Earlier developments

In addition to filming in Rochdale and Manchester, some scenes were shot in Argyll, Scotland, which served as a stand-in for the Western Isles. The decision to film on the mainland was reportedly due to logistical considerations. Highlands and Islands Conservative MSP Jamie McGrigor previously noted that some people were disappointed by the filming location choice but expressed hope that it would boost tourism in the region.

A crime drama called 'From Darkness', which was filmed in Rochdale, is now streaming for free on U. The series originally aired on the BBC in 2015 and follows former police officer Claire Church, portrayed by Anne-Marie Duff, as she grapples with an unresolved serial killer case and her own troubled past.

In addition to Duff and Harris, the cast includes notable actors such as Caroline Lee-Johnson, Luke Newberry, Leanne Best, Richard Rankin, Emma Edmonson, Lyndsey Marshal, Adrian Rawlins, and Josh Taylor. The plot thickens as Claire investigates two victims found at a Manchester building site, linking them to the serial killer she once pursued.

'From Darkness' has received positive reviews on social media and IMDb, with viewers praising its engaging storyline and performances. The series consists of four one-hour episodes, making it accessible for binge-watching. It is currently available for streaming on U.

articles.title: Mahmood Faces British Pakistani Revolt Over Rochdale Rapist Sanctions
articles.excerpt: The British Pakistani community expresses discontent regarding sanctions imposed on a Rochdale rapist, prompting a response from MP Mahmood.
articles.content_html:
A backlash has emerged from the British Pakistani community regarding sanctions imposed on a Rochdale rapist. The controversy centres on the actions taken by authorities in response to the case, which has drawn significant attention.

Local MP Mahmood faces criticism from constituents who feel the measures are inadequate. The community's response highlights concerns over the handling of the case and its implications for public safety.

The sanctions have sparked discussions about accountability and justice within the community, with many calling for more stringent measures against offenders.

As the debate unfolds, it remains to be seen how local authorities will respond to the community's demands.

articles.title: Edenfield Road to Close for Roadworks from 15 August 2026
articles.excerpt: Edenfield Road in Rochdale will be temporarily closed for roadworks starting on 15 August 2026, affecting traffic flow in the area.
articles.content_html:
Part of Edenfield Road will be temporarily closed from 15 August 2026. The closure will begin 10 metres west of the junction with Overtown Lane and extend 674 metres in an easterly direction.

The roadworks are expected to impact traffic flow on Edenfield Road and surrounding routes, including Spotland Road, Sandy Lane, and Bury Road.

Drivers may need to seek alternative routes during this period. Further details about the roadworks and affected areas can be found on the Rochdale Borough Council website.

Residents and commuters are advised to plan their journeys accordingly as the road closure may lead to delays.

articles.title: New Contractor Appointed for Touchstones Rochdale Redevelopment
articles.excerpt: Schofield and Sons has been appointed to continue the redevelopment of Touchstones Rochdale, with work on the first floor galleries and roof repairs set to begin ahead of a planned reopening in summer 2027.
articles.content_html:
A new contractor has been appointed to oversee the next phase of the transformation of Touchstones Rochdale, with Schofield and Sons beginning work on the first floor galleries and roof repairs this month. The redevelopment is part of a multi-million pound project aimed at modernising the venue, which is expected to reopen in summer 2027.

The Greater Manchester construction company will refurbish the first floor gallery spaces, enhancing conditions for artwork and creating brighter, more modern areas for visitors. This phase follows earlier works completed by H H Smith and Sons Ltd, which included the renovation of the ground floor, basement, and initial roof repairs. Those works introduced new flexible performance spaces, a 130-seat studio, meeting rooms, and facilities for education and collections.

Councillor Sue Smith, cabinet member for communities and co-operation at Rochdale Borough Council, expressed enthusiasm for the project, stating, "Appointing Schofield and Sons is another milestone as this project moves into its final year. I am excited to see the improvements to the gallery spaces over the coming months." The redevelopment is funded by the Cultural Development Fund, administered by Arts Council England, alongside contributions from Rochdale Borough Council and The Wolfson Foundation.

The Touchstones project is part of a broader initiative to establish a creative district in Rochdale town centre, which includes the restoration of Rochdale Town Hall and the development of new public art and creative workspaces. The project aims to enhance cultural opportunities for residents and visitors alike, ensuring the venue is fit for the 21st century.

articles.title: Hathershaw College Outlines Plans for New School Building During MP Visit
articles.excerpt: Hathershaw College has outlined its plans for a new school building during a visit from MP Jim McMahon, aiming to enhance facilities while preserving historic features.
articles.content_html:
Hathershaw College has presented its plans for a new school building during a visit from Jim McMahon, the MP for Oldham West, Chadderton and Royton. The school aims to provide modern learning facilities to support its ambition of helping every pupil succeed.

During the visit, principal Susie Fraser and senior leaders discussed the importance of retaining key historic features of the existing building, recognising the school's longstanding role within the local community. Hathershaw College is currently the only secondary school in Oldham that has not benefited from a new building.

Principal Fraser expressed her pleasure in welcoming Mr McMahon and sharing the school's aspirations. She highlighted the need for a building that aligns with the hopes and aspirations for the next generation of students, while also preserving elements of the school's history.

Mr McMahon acknowledged the school's proud history and the need for a learning environment that meets the high aspirations of staff and pupils. He expressed his support for the school's bid for a new building, which he believes would be a significant investment in the future of local young people. Nick Lowry, chief executive of The Pinnacle Learning Trust, also spoke about the importance of modern facilities and the school's heritage, stating that a new building would transform the learning environment for pupils.

articles.title: Rochdale Hornets Defeat Bramley in 1973/74 Players No 6 Trophy Semi-Final
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Hornets secured a 14-2 victory over Bramley in the semi-final of the 1973/74 Players No 6 Trophy at Athletic Ground.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Hornets triumphed over Bramley with a score of 14-2 in the semi-final of the 1973/74 Players No 6 Trophy.

The match took place at Athletic Ground, showcasing the Hornets' strong performance.

This victory advances Rochdale Hornets further in the competition, highlighting their competitive edge this season.

Fans of the Hornets will be eager to see how the team progresses in the tournament following this decisive win.

articles.title: Croft Shifa Pharmacy in Rochdale Sold to First-Time Buyer
articles.excerpt: Croft Shifa Pharmacy, located on Belfield Road, has been sold to first-time buyer Hossiendad Hossieni, marking a significant change in ownership for the local health centre pharmacy.
articles.content_html:
Croft Shifa Pharmacy, located on Belfield Road, has been sold to a first-time buyer following a deal completed by specialist advisers. The pharmacy dispenses an average of 6,400 items each month and operates as a standard-hours health centre pharmacy.

The new owner, Hossiendad Hossieni of NHSR Limited, recognised the potential of the pharmacy, which has been run under full management for some time. Tom Young, senior business agent at Christie & Co, who handled the sale, expressed confidence in Hossieni's ability to grow the business further.

Young noted that Greater Manchester remains a hub for pharmacy transactions, with high demand from various buyer types. He stated that Croft Shifa Pharmacy presented a great opportunity for a first-time buyer and operator to enter a pharmacy that has been well-managed.

The sale is expected to enhance the pharmacy's integration with the health centre, as Hossieni aims to improve the capture rate from the facility. Young wished Hossieni the best for the future, stating he has no doubt Hossieni will take the business to the next level.

articles.title: Rochdale Launches Drug and Alcohol Diversity Programme
articles.excerpt: A new programme aimed at addressing drug and alcohol issues in Rochdale has been launched by Action Together.
articles.content_html:
A new programme aimed at addressing drug and alcohol issues in Rochdale has been launched by Action Together. The initiative promotes diversity and inclusivity in support services for individuals affected by substance misuse.

The Rochdale Drug and Alcohol Diversity Programme will provide tailored support to meet the varied needs of the community. It aims to engage with different cultural groups to ensure that services are accessible and effective.

Action Together stated that the programme will involve collaboration with local organisations and stakeholders to enhance the support network available to residents.

The launch of the programme is likely to improve access to vital resources for those struggling with addiction in Rochdale.

articles.title: Andys Man Club Attends Falinge Park Classic Car and Bike Show
articles.excerpt: Members of Andys Man Club Rochdale were guests at the Falinge Park Classic Car and Bike Show, where they met the newly appointed Mayor Faisal Rana.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 20 July 2026 at 00:07. 2 source updates have been combined into this article.

Members of Andys Man Club Rochdale were guests at the Falinge Park Classic Car and Bike Show, where they met the newly appointed Mayor Faisal Rana.

Latest update

Members of Andys Man Club Rochdale attended the Falinge Park Classic Car and Bike Show as guests of the Rotary Club of Rochdale. The event provided an opportunity for the group to engage with the community and promote their initiatives.

During the show, they met the newly appointed Mayor Faisal Rana and his wife, who expressed their support for the work of Andys Man Club. The club focuses on mental health awareness and support for men in the community.

The event showcased a variety of classic cars and bikes, attracting enthusiasts and families alike. It served as a platform for local organisations to connect with residents and share information about their activities.

Andys Man Club Rochdale continues to encourage open discussions about mental health and aims to reduce stigma surrounding these issues. The support from local leaders like Mayor Rana is seen as vital for their ongoing efforts.

Earlier developments

Members of Andys Man Club Rochdale attended the Falinge Park Classic Car and Bike Show, hosted by the Rotary Club of Rochdale. The event showcased a variety of classic cars and bikes, attracting enthusiasts from the local area.

During the show, members met the newly appointed Mayor of Rochdale, Faisal Rana, and his wife. The Mayor expressed support for the initiatives of Andys Man Club, which focuses on men's mental health and wellbeing.

The presence of Andys Man Club at the event highlights the group's commitment to raising awareness about mental health issues among men in the community. Their participation in local events fosters connections and encourages open discussions.

The Falinge Park Classic Car and Bike Show provided an opportunity for local organisations to engage with the community and promote their causes. Events like this are important for building community spirit and support.

articles.title: Judges Visit Rochdale for RHS Competition
articles.excerpt: Judges from the Royal Horticultural Society toured Rochdale, including key sites such as Castleton train station and Denehurst Park, as part of a local competition.
articles.content_html:
Judges from the Royal Horticultural Society (RHS) visited Rochdale on 21 July as part of a local competition. The judges were taken on a route that included notable locations such as Castleton train station, Denehurst Park, and Rochdale town centre.

The visit aimed to assess the town's floral displays and community efforts in horticulture. Local groups, including Rochdale in Bloom, contributed to the preparations for the judges' tour.

The RHS competition encourages towns to enhance their green spaces and promote community involvement in gardening and environmental initiatives. The results of the competition will be announced later this year.

This event highlights Rochdale's commitment to improving its public spaces and fostering community spirit through gardening and environmental stewardship.

articles.title: Final Approval Granted for 158-Home Development in Castleton
articles.excerpt: Rochdale council has granted final approval for a 158-home development in Castleton, allowing construction to begin on the affordable housing scheme.
articles.content_html:
A 158-home development in Castleton has received final approval from Rochdale council, enabling construction to commence on the affordable housing scheme. The project will feature a mix of two and three-bedroom houses built on Trub Farm, located off Manchester Road.

MCI Developments will transform the land, which is bounded by the canal and situated near Castleton town centre, into a residential area. The plans include private gardens, a children’s play park, and public open space. All homes will be offered at an affordable rate, priced at up to 80 per cent of market value.

The development will consist primarily of semi-detached houses and small terraces, with 48 apartments spread across two three-storey blocks. Access to the site will be via Trub Road, featuring a bridge and walkway over the canal to connect pedestrians to the existing canal footpath. A proposed stairway to the north of the site will also facilitate easy access to Castleton.

The planning committee initially approved the scheme in July 2025, but the local authority has only recently finalised details with the developer regarding the management of public spaces and financial contributions to local infrastructure. Planning documents describe the project as a well-designed neighbourhood that will enhance the area and its surroundings.

articles.title: Police Seek Help to Locate Wanted Man from Rochdale Area
articles.excerpt: Officers are looking for Caylum Smith, who is wanted for failing to appear in court and burglary. He is believed to frequent several areas including Blackley and Salford.
articles.content_html:
Officers are appealing for the public's help to trace a wanted man from the Rochdale area. Caylum Smith, born on 6 August 1997, is wanted for failure to appear at court and burglary.

Smith is believed to frequent the Charleston, Blackley, and Salford areas. Police have urged anyone who may have seen him or has information regarding his whereabouts to come forward.

The appeal comes as part of ongoing efforts to locate individuals wanted by the police in connection with various offences. Authorities are keen to resolve this matter swiftly.

Anyone with information can find Greater Manchester Police and Crimestoppers contact details at the end of this article.

articles.title: Roadworks on Birch Road in Wardle Scheduled for July 2026
articles.excerpt: Roadworks on Birch Road in Wardle will take place from 20 July 2026, resulting in temporary closures and restrictions.
articles.content_html:
A roadworks order will be implemented on Birch Road in Wardle from 20 July 2026. The works are expected to take place between 8am and 6pm on two days, which will be confirmed based on weather conditions.

The closure will affect part of Birch Road, starting at the junction with Ramsden Road and extending 1,236 metres in a south-easterly direction. During this period, no waiting will be permitted in the affected area.

Advance warning signs will be displayed on site two weeks prior to the commencement of the works, indicating the exact dates. Residents and motorists are advised to plan their journeys accordingly during this time.

Alternative routes will be available via Ramsden Road, Wardle Road, and Halifax Road (A58). For further details, a map of roadworks across Greater Manchester can be viewed online.

articles.title: Tram Improvement Works on Rochdale Line Scheduled for 5 September
articles.excerpt: Improvement works on the Rochdale line will disrupt tram services on 5 September, with bus replacements in place until 3pm.
articles.content_html:
On Saturday 5 September, improvement works will take place on the Rochdale line from the start of service until 2pm. During this time, no trams will operate between Freehold and Rochdale Town Centre.

Bus replacement services will run between the affected stops until 3pm. Passengers should be aware that it may take longer for services to reform on the Rochdale line following the part-closure, which could result in additional waiting times for a service.

From 2pm, tram services will resume towards Rochdale Town Centre, although it may take some time for services to fully reform. Frequent Bee Network bus services will also call at the bus stops near each tram stop, potentially offering faster or cheaper journey options depending on the destination.

Passengers are advised that tram tickets will only be accepted on the replacement bus and will not be valid on numbered bus services. Further details will be provided in due course.

articles.title: Rochdale Launches Borough-Wide Conversation Initiative
articles.excerpt: Rochdale has initiated a borough-wide conversation called Round Here, inviting residents to share their ideas and perspectives on the future of the area.
articles.content_html:
A new initiative called Round Here has been launched in Rochdale, aimed at engaging residents in discussions about the future of the borough. The initiative encourages people to share their ideas and perspectives, highlighting the importance of community involvement in shaping local development.

The organisers of Round Here emphasise that no single person or organisation can dictate the future of a place. Instead, they believe that a collective effort involving numerous voices and ideas is essential for building a comprehensive vision for what comes next in Rochdale.

Residents are invited to participate in this conversation, which seeks to foster collaboration and dialogue among the community. The initiative aims to create a platform where diverse opinions can be expressed and considered in future planning and decision-making processes.

This effort reflects a growing recognition of the value of community engagement in local governance, as stakeholders work together to address the challenges and opportunities facing Rochdale.

articles.excerpt: A new Rock Choir group has launched in Rochdale, offering free taster sessions for those interested in joining.

A new Rock Choir group has launched in Rochdale, providing free taster sessions for anyone interested in joining. The sessions aim to encourage community participation and allow potential members to experience the choir's activities before committing to membership.

Participants can expect a welcoming environment where they can explore singing in a group setting. The choir is designed for people of all abilities, making it accessible to anyone who enjoys music.

The free taster sessions will take place at various times, allowing flexibility for those interested. Further details about the schedule and how to join can be obtained from the choir's official channels.

This initiative is part of a broader effort to enhance community engagement through music and the arts in Rochdale.

articles.title: Rochdale Council Proposes New Dog Fouling Public Space Protection Order
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Borough Council has proposed a new Public Space Protection Order aimed at tackling dog fouling in the area, following concerns over cleanliness and public health.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Borough Council has proposed a new Public Space Protection Order (PSPO) to address dog fouling in the area. The initiative aims to improve cleanliness and public health.

The proposed PSPO is in accordance with the Anti-Social Behaviour, Crime and Policing Act 2014, Section 59. It seeks to provide the council with additional powers to manage dog fouling effectively.

If implemented, the order could lead to increased fines for dog owners who fail to clean up after their pets.

The council is currently seeking feedback on the proposal, with further details available in the document published on their website.

articles.title: Five men arrested in Rochdale during counter-terrorism raid
articles.excerpt: Counter-terrorism police arrested five men in Rochdale, four of whom are Iranian nationals, in connection with a suspected terrorist plot.
articles.content_html:
Counter-terrorism police arrested five men in Rochdale on Sunday in connection with a suspected terrorist plot. The operation involved members of the 22 Special Air Service and Counter Terrorist Specialist Firearms Officers, who detained the men during a raid.

Four of the arrested men are Iranian nationals. The police have not disclosed further details regarding the nature of the suspected plot or the identities of those arrested.

The arrests took place at a residential property in the Rochdale area, where officers were seen detaining a suspect. The police operation was part of an ongoing investigation into potential terrorist activities.

Authorities have not yet provided information on any charges or court appearances related to the arrests. The investigation is ongoing, and further updates are expected in due course.

articles.title: Heywood Students Visit First Choice Homes Oldham as Part of Summer School
articles.excerpt: Three teenagers from the Heywood and Middleton North constituency participated in a two-day programme with First Choice Homes Oldham, gaining insights into the housing association's operations.
articles.content_html:
Three teenagers from the Heywood and Middleton North constituency spent two days with First Choice Homes Oldham as part of MP Elsie Blundell's Fly High Summer School. Emaan Hassan, Caryn-Ann Sarpong-Kumankomah and Charlie Wilcock, all aged 16, were mentored by chief executive Mark Gifford and members of the senior leadership team.

During their visit, the students engaged with various departments, including development, neighbourhood services, sustainability, and the customer contact centre. This experience provided them with insights into the work of the housing association and its role in supporting communities across Oldham.

First Choice Homes Oldham also donated £300 to the Fly High Summer School to support the group's visit to the Houses of Parliament in London. Reflecting on the experience, Emaan Hassan noted the size of the organisation and the number of employees involved. Caryn-Ann Sarpong-Kumankomah found the meeting with the chief executive particularly beneficial, while Charlie Wilcock expressed surprise at the scale of social housing managed by the organisation.

Mark Gifford stated that the organisation was pleased to host the students and highlighted the importance of such initiatives in providing young people with insights into local democracy. MP Elsie Blundell expressed gratitude for the warm welcome the students received and emphasised the value of partnerships in creating opportunities for young people.

articles.title: Calls for Crackdown on HMOs in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: Concerns have been raised regarding the proliferation of Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs) in Rochdale, prompting calls for stricter regulations.
articles.content_html:
Concerns have been raised about the increasing number of Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs) in Rochdale. Local residents have expressed worries regarding the impact of these properties on the community.

Calls for a crackdown on HMOs have intensified, with some residents advocating for stricter regulations to manage their growth. They argue that the rise in HMOs has led to issues such as overcrowding.

Local councillors are reportedly discussing potential measures to address these concerns, including reviewing planning policies related to HMOs. The aim is to balance affordable housing with community standards.

Further discussions are expected in upcoming council meetings as residents continue to voice their concerns.

articles.content_html:
A local leader has sent a letter to Rachel Laver, the new Chief Executive of the Rochdale Development Agency, outlining priorities for growing the local economy.

The letter highlights the importance of skills and training to provide young people and workers with future opportunities. It also emphasises the need for eco-friendly initiatives to support sustainable development in the area.

The leader's proposals aim to address current economic challenges and foster a more resilient local economy.

articles.title: Rochdale Masonic Hall Celebrates Centenary
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Masonic Hall marks its centenary with a special ceremony, including a re-enactment of the foundation stone laying.
articles.content_html:
A ceremony to celebrate the centenary of Rochdale Masonic Hall took place recently, featuring a re-enactment of the laying of the foundation stone.

The event was organised by the Provincial Grand Lodge of East Lancashire, highlighting the historical significance of the hall in the community.

Attendees were able to witness the traditional ceremony, which included an explanation of its importance and the rituals involved.

The centenary celebration reflects the hall's long-standing presence and role within Rochdale's social fabric.

articles.title: Voter ID Required for GM Mayoral Election in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Borough Council reminds voters that photo ID is necessary for the upcoming GM Mayoral Election on 30 July. Those without ID can apply for a free Voter Authority Certificate before 22 July.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Borough Council has announced that voters will need an accepted form of photo identification to vote at polling stations during the Greater Manchester Mayoral Election on 30 July.

Residents without an accepted form of ID are encouraged to apply for a free Voter Authority Certificate. Applications must be submitted before 5pm on 22 July to ensure eligibility to vote.

The council has provided information on how to obtain the Voter Authority Certificate, which can be found on their official website.

This requirement is part of new regulations aimed at ensuring the integrity of the voting process in the upcoming election.

articles.title: Traffic Updates and Local News from Rochdale
articles.excerpt: Local MPs for Rochdale and Bury North have expressed their support for Prime Minister Andy Burnham's inaugural speech, which outlined plans to address rough sleeping and alleviate cost of living pressures. The Prime Minister also presented a long-term programme for government. Hathershaw College hosted Jim McMahon, MP for Oldham West, Chadderton and Royton…
articles.content_html:
Local MPs for Rochdale and Bury North have expressed their support for Prime Minister Andy Burnham's inaugural speech, which outlined plans to address rough sleeping and alleviate cost of living pressures. The Prime Minister also presented a long-term programme for government.

Hathershaw College hosted Jim McMahon, MP for Oldham West, Chadderton and Royton, to discuss its plans for a new school building and its vision for the future. Meanwhile, Reform UK's candidate for Greater Manchester Mayor has pledged support for a campaign aimed at reopening Bury Castle Armoury.

In community news, Rock Choir has launched a new group in Rochdale, offering local residents the opportunity to join the contemporary singing organisation without the need for auditions or music reading skills.

Additionally, a Bury Council environmental protection officer has been recognised in the ENDS Report Power List 2026 for her contributions to contaminated land management. Three teenagers from the Heywood and Middleton North constituency participated in a two-day programme with First Choice Homes Oldham as part of MP Elsie Blundell's Fly High Summer School.

articles.title: Rochdale Councillors Oppose Pay Rise Amid Criticism of Labour Party
articles.excerpt: Councillors in Rochdale have taken a stand against proposed pay rises for themselves, amid ongoing criticism of the Labour Party's priorities.
articles.content_html:
Councillors in Rochdale have opposed proposed pay rises for themselves, stating their commitment to serving the community rather than seeking financial gain.

The decision comes amid criticism directed at the Labour Party, with claims that the party prioritises financial benefits over the needs of voters.

This stance reflects a broader sentiment among some local representatives who believe that public service should not be financially motivated.

The implications of this decision may influence public perception of the council and its members, particularly in light of ongoing discussions about local governance and accountability.
